mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows and forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation confirms capacity utilization improvements that boost profit margins and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO accelerates under low inflation, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0 percent triggers liquidity constraints that compress valuation multiples and force immediate negative equity returns within the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that raises wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a positive mean forward return over 60 days, contradicting the cost-push inflation mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows and GDPC1 growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high unemployment signals a deepening economic contraction that immediately triggers defensive equity selling over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases when INDPRO declines while UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.
